verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem quidam languens Lazarus a Bethania, de castello Mariæ et Marthæ sororis ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δέ τις ἀσθενῶν λάζαρος ἀπὸ βηθανίας ἐκ τῆς κώμης μαρίας καὶ μάρθας τῆς ἀδελφῆς αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: Now a certain *man* was sick, *named* Lazarus, of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Martha.

verses.latin_vulgate: (Maria autem erat quæ unxit Dominum unguento, et extersit pedes ejus capillis suis: cujus frater Lazarus infirmabatur.)
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ μαρία ἡ ἀλείψασα τὸν κύριον μύρῳ καὶ ἐκμάξασα τοὺς πόδας αὐτοῦ ταῖς θριξὶν αὐτῆς ἡς ὁ ἀδελφὸς λάζαρος ἠσθένει
verses.king_james: (It was *that* Mary which anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.)

verses.latin_vulgate: Miserunt ergo sorores ejus ad eum dicentes: Domine, ecce quem amas infirmatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπέστειλαν οὖν αἱ ἀδελφαὶ πρὸς αὐτὸν λέγουσαι κύριε ἴδε ὃν φιλεῖς ἀσθενεῖ
verses.king_james: Therefore his sisters sent unto him, saying, Lord, behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audiens autem Jesus dixit eis: Infirmitas hæc non est ad mortem, sed pro gloria Dei, ut glorificetur Filius Dei per eam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσας δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὕτη ἡ ἀσθένεια οὐκ ἔστιν πρὸς θάνατον ἀλλ᾽ ὑπὲρ τῆς δόξης τοῦ θεοῦ ἵνα δοξασθῇ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ δι᾽ αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: When Jesus heard *that*, he said, This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.

verses.latin_vulgate: Diligebat autem Jesus Martham, et sororem ejus Mariam, et Lazarum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἠγάπα δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς τὴν μάρθαν καὶ τὴν ἀδελφὴν αὐτῆς καὶ τὸν λάζαρον
verses.king_james: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut ergo audivit quia infirmabatur, tunc quidem mansit in eodem loco duobus diebus;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς οὖν ἤκουσεν ὅτι ἀσθενεῖ τότε μὲν ἔμεινεν ἐν ᾧ ἦν τόπῳ δύο ἡμέρας
verses.king_james: When he had heard therefore that he was sick, he abode two days still in the same place where he was.

verses.latin_vulgate: deinde post hæc dixit discipulis suis: Eamus in Judæam iterum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔπειτα μετὰ τοῦτο λέγει τοῖς μαθηταῖς ἄγωμεν εἰς τὴν ἰουδαίαν πάλιν
verses.king_james: Then after that saith he to *his* disciples, Let us go into Judaea again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicunt ei discipuli: Rabbi, nunc quærebant te Judæi lapidare, et iterum vadis illuc?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγουσιν αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταί ῥαββί νῦν ἐζήτουν σε λιθάσαι οἱ ἰουδαῖοι καὶ πάλιν ὑπάγεις ἐκεῖ
verses.king_james: *His* disciples say unto him, Master, the Jews of late sought to stone thee; and goest thou thither again?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus: Nonne duodecim sunt horæ diei? Si quis ambulaverit in die, non offendit, quia lucem hujus mundi videt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς οὐχὶ δώδεκα εἰσιν ὧραί τῆς ἡμέρας ἐάν τις περιπατῇ ἐν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ οὐ προσκόπτει ὅτι τὸ φῶς τοῦ κόσμου τούτου βλέπει
verses.king_james: Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world.

verses.latin_vulgate: si autem ambulaverit in nocte, offendit, quia lux non est in eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν δέ τις περιπατῇ ἐν τῇ νυκτί προσκόπτει ὅτι τὸ φῶς οὐκ ἔστιν ἐν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc ait, et post hæc dixit eis: Lazarus amicus noster dormit: sed vado ut a somno excitem eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα εἶπεν καὶ μετὰ τοῦτο λέγει αὐτοῖς λάζαρος ὁ φίλος ἡμῶν κεκοίμηται ἀλλὰ πορεύομαι ἵνα ἐξυπνίσω αὐτόν
verses.king_james: These things said he: and after that he saith unto them, Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt ergo discipuli ejus: Domine, si dormit, salvus erit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον οὖν οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ, κύριε εἰ κεκοίμηται σωθήσεται
verses.king_james: Then said his disciples, Lord, if he sleep, he shall do well.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerat autem Jesus de morte ejus: illi autem putaverunt quia de dormitione somni diceret.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰρήκει δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς περὶ τοῦ θανάτου αὐτοῦ ἐκεῖνοι δὲ ἔδοξαν ὅτι περὶ τῆς κοιμήσεως τοῦ ὕπνου λέγει
verses.king_james: Howbeit Jesus spake of his death: but they thought that he had spoken of taking of rest in sleep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc ergo Jesus dixit eis manifeste: Lazarus mortuus est:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε οὖν εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς παῤῥησίᾳ λάζαρος ἀπέθανεν
verses.king_james: Then said Jesus unto them plainly, Lazarus is dead.

verses.latin_vulgate: et gaudeo propter vos, ut credatis, quoniam non eram ibi, sed eamus ad eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ χαίρω δι᾽ ὑμᾶς ἵνα πιστεύσητε ὅτι οὐκ ἤμην ἐκεῖ ἀλλ᾽ ἄγωμεν πρὸς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo Thomas, qui dicitur Didymus, ad condiscipulos: Eamus et nos, ut moriamur cum eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν οὖν θωμᾶς ὁ λεγόμενος δίδυμος τοῖς συμμαθηταῖς ἄγωμεν καὶ ἡμεῖς ἵνα ἀποθάνωμεν μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Then said Thomas, which is called Didymus, unto his fellowdisciples, Let us also go, that we may die with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit itaque Jesus: et invenit eum quatuor dies jam in monumento habentem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλθὼν οὖν ὁ ἰησοῦς εὗρεν αὐτὸν τέσσαρας ἡμέρας ἤδη ἔχοντα ἐν τῷ μνημείῳ
verses.king_james: Then when Jesus came, he found that he had *lain* in the grave four days already.

verses.latin_vulgate: (Erat autem Bethania juxta Jerosolymam quasi stadiis quindecim.)
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ἡ βηθανία ἐγγὺς τῶν ἱεροσολύμων ὡς ἀπὸ σταδίων δεκαπέντε
verses.king_james: Now Bethany was nigh unto Jerusalem, about fifteen furlongs off:

verses.latin_vulgate: Multi autem ex Judæis venerant ad Martham et Mariam, ut consolarentur eas de fratre suo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πολλοὶ ἐκ τῶν ἰουδαίων ἐληλύθεισαν πρὸς τὰς περὶ μάρθαν καὶ μαρίαν, ἵνα παραμυθήσωνται αὐτὰς περὶ τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And many of the Jews came to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Martha ergo ut audivit quia Jesus venit, occurrit illi: Maria autem domi sedebat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ οὖν μάρθα ὡς ἤκουσεν ὅτι ὁ ἰησοῦς ἔρχεται ὑπήντησεν αὐτῷ μαρία δὲ ἐν τῷ οἴκῳ ἐκαθέζετο
verses.king_james: Then Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, went and met him: but Mary sat *still* in the house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo Martha ad Jesum: Domine, si fuisses hic, frater meus non fuisset mortuus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν οὖν ἡ μάρθα πρὸς τὸν ἰησοῦν κύριε εἰ ἦς ὧδε ὁ ἀδελφός μου οὐκ ἂν ἐτεθνήκει
verses.king_james: Then said Martha unto Jesus, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.

verses.latin_vulgate: sed et nunc scio quia quæcumque poposceris a Deo, dabit tibi Deus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ καὶ νῦν οἶδα ὅτι ὅσα ἂν αἰτήσῃ τὸν θεὸν δώσει σοι ὁ θεός
verses.king_james: But I know, that even now, whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, God will give *it* thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit illi Jesus: Resurget frater tuus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῇ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἀναστήσεται ὁ ἀδελφός σου
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto her, Thy brother shall rise again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Martha: Scio quia resurget in resurrectione in novissimo die.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ μάρθα οἶδα ὅτι ἀναστήσεται ἐν τῇ ἀναστάσει ἐν τῇ ἐσχάτῃ ἡμέρᾳ
verses.king_james: Martha saith unto him, I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection at the last day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ei Jesus: Ego sum resurrectio et vita: qui credit in me, etiam si mortuus fuerit, vivet:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν αὐτῇ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ ἀνάστασις καὶ ἡ ζωή ὁ πιστεύων εἰς ἐμὲ κἂν ἀποθάνῃ ζήσεται
verses.king_james: Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live:

verses.latin_vulgate: et omnis qui vivit et credit in me, non morietur in æternum. Credis hoc?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πᾶς ὁ ζῶν καὶ πιστεύων εἰς ἐμὲ οὐ μὴ ἀποθάνῃ εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα πιστεύεις τοῦτο
verses.king_james: And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die. Believest thou this?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illi: Utique Domine, ego credidi quia tu es Christus, Filius Dei vivi, qui in hunc mundum venisti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ναί κύριε ἐγὼ πεπίστευκα ὅτι σὺ εἶ ὁ χριστὸς ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ ὁ εἰς τὸν κόσμον ἐρχόμενος
verses.king_james: She saith unto him, Yea, Lord: I believe that thou art the Christ, the Son of God, which should come into the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum hæc dixisset, abiit, et vocavit Mariam sororem suam silentio, dicens: Magister adest, et vocat te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ταῦτά εἰποῦσα ἀπῆλθεν καὶ ἐφώνησεν μαρίαν τὴν ἀδελφὴν αὐτῆς λάθρᾳ εἰποῦσα ὁ διδάσκαλος πάρεστιν καὶ φωνεῖ σε
verses.king_james: And when she had so said, she went her way, and called Mary her sister secretly, saying, The Master is come, and calleth for thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Illa ut audivit, surgit cito, et venit ad eum;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκείνη ὡς ἤκουσεν ἐγείρεται ταχὺ καὶ ἔρχεται πρὸς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: As soon as she heard *that*, she arose quickly, and came unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: nondum enim venerat Jesus in castellum: sed erat adhuc in illo loco, ubi occurrerat ei Martha.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὔπω δὲ ἐληλύθει ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰς τὴν κώμην ἀλλ᾽ ἦν ἐν τῷ τόπῳ ὅπου ὑπήντησεν αὐτῷ ἡ μάρθα
verses.king_james: Now Jesus was not yet come into the town, but was in that place where Martha met him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Judæi ergo, qui erant cum ea in domo, et consolabantur eam, cum vidissent Mariam quia cito surrexit, et exiit, secuti sunt eam dicentes: Quia vadit ad monumentum, ut ploret ibi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ οὖν ἰουδαῖοι οἱ ὄντες μετ᾽ αὐτῆς ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ καὶ παραμυθούμενοι αὐτήν ἰδόντες τὴν μαρίαν, ὅτι ταχέως ἀνέστη καὶ ἐξῆλθεν ἠκολούθησαν αὐτῇ λέγοντες, ὅτι ὑπάγει εἰς τὸ μνημεῖον ἵνα κλαύσῃ ἐκεῖ
verses.king_james: The Jews then which were with her in the house, and comforted her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up hastily and went out, followed her, saying, She goeth unto the grave to weep there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Maria ergo, cum venisset ubi erat Jesus, videns eum, cecidit ad pedes ejus, et dicit ei: Domine, si fuisses hic, non esset mortuus frater meus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ οὖν μαρία, ὡς ἦλθεν ὅπου ἦν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἰδοῦσα αὐτὸν ἔπεσεν εἰς τοὺς πόδας αὐτοῦ λέγουσα αὐτῷ κύριε εἰ ἦς ὧδε οὐκ ἄν ἀπέθανεν μου ὁ ἀδελφός
verses.king_james: Then when Mary was come where Jesus was, and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying unto him, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus ergo, ut vidit eam plorantem, et Judæos, qui venerant cum ea, plorantes, infremuit spiritu, et turbavit seipsum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰησοῦς οὖν ὡς εἶδεν αὐτὴν κλαίουσαν καὶ τοὺς συνελθόντας αὐτῇ ἰουδαίους κλαίοντας ἐνεβριμήσατο τῷ πνεύματι καὶ ἐτάραξεν ἑαυτόν
verses.king_james: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews also weeping which came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled,

verses.latin_vulgate: et dixit: Ubi posuistis eum? Dicunt ei: Domine, veni, et vide.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν ποῦ τεθείκατε αὐτόν λέγουσιν αὐτῷ κύριε ἔρχου καὶ ἴδε
verses.king_james: And said, Where have ye laid him? They said unto him, Lord, come and see.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et lacrimatus est Jesus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐδάκρυσεν ὁ ἰησοῦς
verses.king_james: Jesus wept.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt ergo Judæi: Ecce quomodo amabat eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγον οὖν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι ἴδε πῶς ἐφίλει αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Then said the Jews, Behold how he loved him!

verses.latin_vulgate: Quidam autem ex ipsis dixerunt: Non poterat hic, qui aperuit oculos cæci nati, facere ut hic non moreretur?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τινὲς δὲ ἐξ αὐτῶν εἶπον, οὐκ ἠδύνατο οὗτος ὁ ἀνοίξας τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς τοῦ τυφλοῦ ποιῆσαι ἵνα καὶ οὗτος μὴ ἀποθάνῃ
verses.king_james: And some of them said, Could not this man, which opened the eyes of the blind, have caused that even this man should not have died?

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus ergo rursum fremens in semetipso, venit ad monumentum. Erat autem spelunca, et lapis superpositus erat ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰησοῦς οὖν πάλιν ἐμβριμώμενος ἐν ἑαυτῷ ἔρχεται εἰς τὸ μνημεῖον ἦν δὲ σπήλαιον καὶ λίθος ἐπέκειτο ἐπ᾽ αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: Jesus therefore again groaning in himself cometh to the grave. It was a cave, and a stone lay upon it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait Jesus: Tollite lapidem. Dicit ei Martha, soror ejus qui mortuus fuerat: Domine, jam fœtet, quatriduanus est enim.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει ὁ ἰησοῦς ἄρατε τὸν λίθον λέγει αὐτῷ ἡ ἀδελφὴ τοῦ τεθνηκότος μάρθα κύριε ἤδη ὄζει τεταρταῖος γάρ ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Jesus said, Take ye away the stone. Martha, the sister of him that was dead, saith unto him, Lord, by this time he stinketh: for he hath been *dead* four days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Jesus: Nonne dixi tibi quoniam si credideris, videbis gloriam Dei?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῇ ὁ ἰησοῦς οὐκ εἶπόν σοι ὅτι ἐὰν πιστεύσῃς ὄψει τὴν δόξαν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto her, Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory of God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulerunt ergo lapidem: Jesus autem, elevatis sursum oculis, dixit: Pater, gratias ago tibi quoniam audisti me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦραν οὖν τὸν λίθον οὗ ἦν ὁ τεθνηκὼς κειμένος ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς ἦρεν τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς ἄνω καὶ εἶπεν πάτερ εὐχαριστῶ σοι ὅτι ἤκουσάς μου
verses.king_james: Then they took away the stone *from the place* where the dead was laid. And Jesus lifted up *his* eyes, and said, Father, I thank thee that thou hast heard me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem sciebam quia semper me audis, sed propter populum qui circumstat, dixi: ut credant quia tu me misisti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ δὲ ᾔδειν ὅτι πάντοτέ μου ἀκούεις ἀλλὰ διὰ τὸν ὄχλον τὸν περιεστῶτα εἶπον ἵνα πιστεύσωσιν ὅτι σύ με ἀπέστειλας
verses.king_james: And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the people which stand by I said *it*, that they may believe that thou hast sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc cum dixisset, voce magna clamavit: Lazare, veni foras.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ταῦτα εἰπὼν φωνῇ μεγάλῃ ἐκραύγασεν λάζαρε δεῦρο ἔξω
verses.king_james: And when he thus had spoken, he cried with a loud voice, Lazarus, come forth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et statim prodiit qui fuerat mortuus, ligatus pedes, et manus institis, et facies illius sudario erat ligata. Dixit eis Jesus: Solvite eum et sinite abire.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξῆλθεν ὁ τεθνηκὼς δεδεμένος τοὺς πόδας καὶ τὰς χεῖρας κειρίαις καὶ ἡ ὄψις αὐτοῦ σουδαρίῳ περιεδέδετο λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς λύσατε αὐτὸν καὶ ἄφετε ὑπάγειν
verses.king_james: And he that was dead came forth, bound hand and foot with graveclothes: and his face was bound about with a napkin. Jesus saith unto them, Loose him, and let him go.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multi ergo ex Judæis, qui venerant ad Mariam, et Martham, et viderant quæ fecit Jesus, crediderunt in eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλοὶ οὖν ἐκ τῶν ἰουδαίων οἱ ἐλθόντες πρὸς τὴν μαρίαν καὶ θεασάμενοι ἃ ἐποίησεν ὁ ἰησοῦς, ἐπίστευσαν εἰς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Then many of the Jews which came to Mary, and had seen the things which Jesus did, believed on him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quidam autem ex ipsis abierunt ad pharisæos, et dixerunt eis quæ fecit Jesus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τινὲς δὲ ἐξ αὐτῶν ἀπῆλθον πρὸς τοὺς φαρισαίους καὶ εἶπον αὐτοῖς ἃ ἐποίησεν ὁ ἰησοῦς
verses.king_james: But some of them went their ways to the Pharisees, and told them what things Jesus had done.

verses.latin_vulgate: Collegerunt ergo pontifices et pharisæi concilium, et dicebant: Quid faciamus, quia hic homo multa signa facit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνήγαγον οὖν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ φαρισαῖοι συνέδριον καὶ ἔλεγον τί ποιοῦμεν ὅτι οὗτος ὁ ἄνθρωπος πολλὰ σημεῖα ποιεῖ
verses.king_james: Then gathered the chief priests and the Pharisees a council, and said, What do we? for this man doeth many miracles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si dimittimus eum sic, omnes credent in eum, et venient Romani, et tollent nostrum locum, et gentem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν ἀφῶμεν αὐτὸν οὕτως πάντες πιστεύσουσιν εἰς αὐτόν καὶ ἐλεύσονται οἱ ῥωμαῖοι καὶ ἀροῦσιν ἡμῶν καὶ τὸν τόπον καὶ τὸ ἔθνος
verses.king_james: If we let him thus alone, all *men* will believe on him: and the Romans shall come and take away both our place and nation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unus autem ex ipsis, Caiphas nomine, cum esset pontifex anni illius, dixit eis: Vos nescitis quidquam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἷς δέ τις ἐξ αὐτῶν καϊάφας ἀρχιερεὺς ὢν τοῦ ἐνιαυτοῦ ἐκείνου εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὑμεῖς οὐκ οἴδατε οὐδέν
verses.king_james: And one of them, *named* Caiaphas, being the high priest that same year, said unto them, Ye know nothing at all,

verses.latin_vulgate: nec cogitatis quia expedit vobis ut unus moriatur homo pro populo, et non tota gens pereat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδὲ διαλογίζεσθε ὅτι συμφέρει ἡμῖν ἵνα εἷς ἄνθρωπος ἀποθάνῃ ὑπὲρ τοῦ λαοῦ καὶ μὴ ὅλον τὸ ἔθνος ἀπόληται
verses.king_james: Nor consider that it is expedient for us, that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation perish not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc autem a semetipso non dixit: sed cum esset pontifex anni illius, prophetavit, quod Jesus moriturus erat pro gente,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο δὲ ἀφ᾽ ἑαυτοῦ οὐκ εἶπεν ἀλλὰ ἀρχιερεὺς ὢν τοῦ ἐνιαυτοῦ ἐκείνου προεφήτευσεν ὅτι ἔμελλεν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἀποθνῄσκειν ὑπὲρ τοῦ ἔθνους
verses.king_james: And this spake he not of himself: but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus should die for that nation;

verses.latin_vulgate: et non tantum pro gente, sed ut filios Dei, qui erant dispersi, congregaret in unum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐχ ὑπὲρ τοῦ ἔθνους μόνον ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα καὶ τὰ τέκνα τοῦ θεοῦ τὰ διεσκορπισμένα συναγάγῃ εἰς ἕν
verses.king_james: And not for that nation only, but that also he should gather together in one the children of God that were scattered abroad.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ab illo ergo die cogitaverunt ut interficerent eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπ᾽ ἐκείνης οὖν τῆς ἡμέρας συνεβουλεύσαντο ἵνα ἀποκτείνωσιν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Then from that day forth they took counsel together for to put him to death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus ergo jam non in palam ambulabat apud Judæos, sed abiit in regionem juxta desertum, in civitatem quæ dicitur Ephrem, et ibi morabatur cum discipulis suis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰησοῦς οὖν οὐκ ἔτι παῤῥησίᾳ περιεπάτει ἐν τοῖς ἰουδαίοις ἀλλὰ ἀπῆλθεν ἐκεῖθεν εἰς τὴν χώραν ἐγγὺς τῆς ἐρήμου εἰς ἐφραὶμ λεγομένην πόλιν κἀκεῖ διέτριβεν μετὰ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews; but went thence unto a country near to the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there continued with his disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Proximum autem erat Pascha Judæorum, et ascenderunt multi Jerosolymam de regione ante Pascha, ut sanctificarent seipsos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ἐγγὺς τὸ πάσχα τῶν ἰουδαίων καὶ ἀνέβησαν πολλοὶ εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα ἐκ τῆς χώρας πρὸ τοῦ πάσχα ἵνα ἁγνίσωσιν ἑαυτούς
verses.king_james: And the Jews' passover was nigh at hand: and many went out of the country up to Jerusalem before the passover, to purify themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quærebant ergo Jesum, et colloquebantur ad invicem, in templo stantes: Quid putatis, quia non venit ad diem festum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐζήτουν οὖν τὸν ἰησοῦν καὶ ἔλεγον μετ᾽ ἀλλήλων ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ ἑστηκότες τί δοκεῖ ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐ μὴ ἔλθῃ εἰς τὴν ἑορτήν
verses.king_james: Then sought they for Jesus, and spake among themselves, as they stood in the temple, What think ye, that he will not come to the feast?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dederant autem pontifices et pharisæi mandatum ut si quis cognoverit ubi sit, indicet, ut apprehendant eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δεδώκεισαν δὲ καὶ οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ φαρισαῖοι ἐντολὴν ἵνα ἐάν τις γνῷ ποῦ ἐστιν μηνύσῃ ὅπως πιάσωσιν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Now both the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a commandment, that, if any man knew where he were, he should shew *it*, that they might take him.
